description:
The typer registers two kinds of specs by two mechanisms:

1. Phantom any-key — `(F, [any; arity])` registered for *every* fn
   unconditionally (ir_typer.rs:246-251 + SCC pending seed at :277-281).
2. Real discovery — SCC walker walks each registered spec's body and
   registers narrow specs for each callsite.

(1) was scaffolding for (2). But its widened body propagates `any`
through arithmetic / list / map ops, generating narrow-looking specs
from a caller that doesn't exist. The `[int|float, int|float]` spec
for `count` in tail_recursion.specs is exactly this leak: under the
any-key body, `n - 1` widens to `int|float`, the recursive
`count(int|float, int|float)` callsite gets faithfully registered,
and the resulting spec is purely synthetic.

The post-pass any-key DROP (.29.12.6 at ir_typer.rs:428-665) cleans
up the dead any-key itself, but it doesn't undo the orphan narrow
specs it spawned. They survive forever — compiled, wasted SpecIds,
visible in golden_specs.

## The right shape

A spec `(F, K)` exists iff some other registered spec's body has a
callsite that reaches `(F, K)`. Seed only from genuine roots:

  - `(main, [])` — program entry (`rt.spawn(main_fn)`).
  - Future Rust-callable entries — enumerated explicitly when added.

Everything else is *discovered* from those seeds by walking
registered spec bodies:

  - Direct `Call/TailCall(F, args)` in spec body → seed `(F, key)`
    where key = current spec's env view of `args`.
  - `MakeClosure(F, captured)` → seed `(F, [captured_descrs..., any...])`.
    The any-fill for arg slots is the proper conservative model
    for "closure value escapes; some unknown CallClosure will
    invoke it later."
  - fn_constants-resolved `CallClosure/TailCallClosure(F, args)` →
    seed `(F, captured_descrs ++ args_descrs)` — TIGHTER than the
    MakeClosure registration, exactly mirrors what the rewrite at
    `rewrite_known_target_closures` produces.
  - Cont site (`Term::Call`/`CallClosure`/`Receive`) → seed
    `(cont.fn_id, cont_input_key(...))`.

Termination: keep widening. Per-SCC, after iter >= WIDEN_AT widen
new keys destined for SCC-internal fns. The same logic
`walk_spec_for_discovery` already uses.

Receive / Spawn coverage:

  - Receive: cont fn_id seeded as a cont site (existing handling).
  - Spawn: target is the lambda of a MakeClosure that flows into
    `Builtin(Spawn, [closure_var])`. The MakeClosure walker
    registers the lambda's spec; Spawn is covered transitively.

## What this fixes (concrete examples)

  - tail_recursion.specs: `count(2)` loses the `[int|float, int|float]`
    pollution spec. Two specs remain: `[100000, 0]` and `[int, int]`.
  - closure_typed_captures.specs: `lambda_3(3)` loses the `[any, any, any]`
    bootstrap spec. One spec remains: `[10, 20, any]`. Codegen drops
    from 2 compiled lambda_3 bodies to 1.
  - higher_order.specs: same pattern for unused any-key bodies of
    closure-reachable fns.
  - Every fn that's only ever directly-called drops its phantom
    any-key. Spec count = real call-graph size.

## Verifications already done

  - codegen tolerates missing any-keys: fz-ul4.29.12.6's
    `register_any_key_at` pads slot F.0 with a sentinel when the
    typer omitted F's any-key. Confirmed: ir_codegen.rs:1806-1815
    + spec_keys filtering at :1847-1864.
  - SpecRegistry.resolve never returns sentinel SpecIds; closure
    / Spawn / Receive paths that index by FnId.0 land on the
    real any-key when present and fall through to a sentinel
    (which the compile loop skips) when absent.
  - by_fn_idx legacy view: only one codegen consumer at
    ir_codegen.rs:2066, which discards the read (`let _ = ft;`).
    Free to retire.
  - spec_check::validate_specs iterates `module_types.specs`; no
    any-key dependency.
  - Spawn from Rust (`Runtime::spawn(fn_id)`): the only call site
    in JIT is `rt.spawn(main_fn)` in main.rs:577. `(main, [])`
    seed covers it. Future Rust-side entry points enumerate
    explicitly.

## DAG of atomic, testable steps

### Step 1 — extract entry-seed set

Add `fn entry_seeds(m: &Module) -> Vec<(FnId, Vec<Descr>)>` in
ir_typer.rs. Returns `[(main_fn_id, vec![])]` if `main` exists, else
empty. Wire it into `type_module` without changing behavior yet:
add the seeds to `pending` alongside the existing per-fn seeds.

Test: golden_specs unchanged (we're only adding redundant seeds the
unconditional bootstrap already covers).

### Step 2 — replace unconditional bootstrap with entry-seed worklist

Remove ir_typer.rs:246-251 (bootstrap any-key insertion) and
:277-281 (per-fn pending seed). Seed `pending` from
`entry_seeds(m)` only.

Tests:
  - `cargo test --lib` — typer unit tests. Several expect the
    bootstrap any-key (e.g., `specs_contains_any_key_for_every_fn`).
    Update them: any-key is registered iff `(F, [any...])` is
    reachable from a seed.
  - `BLESS_SPECS=1 cargo test golden_specs` then EYEBALL each
    diff. Acceptable diffs: any-key specs disappear for fns that
    are direct-call-only. Unacceptable: previously-narrow specs
    disappear, or specs widen, or main/entry fns lose their key.
  - `cargo test` full — codegen tests should pass unchanged.
    `lambda_3` compiles 1 body instead of 2; SpecRegistry's
    sentinel padding for the missing any-key kicks in (already
    supported by .29.12.6).

### Step 3 — drop the post-pass any-key drop (now redundant)

Remove ir_typer.rs:428-665 (the `.29.12.6` drop pass). Under the
new model, orphan any-keys were never registered, so there's
nothing to drop.

Tests:
  - Full test suite. If anything fails, an any-key was being
    drop-pass-cleaned that step 2's seed model didn't account
    for — diagnose, augment seeds, retest.
  - golden_specs unchanged from end of step 2.

### Step 4 — retire `by_fn_idx` (cleanup)

`ModuleTypes::by_fn_idx`, `Index<usize>`, `len`, `iter` (legacy
view; one codegen consumer at ir_codegen.rs:2066 already discards
its read via `let _ = ft;`).

Replace `ModuleTypes::Index<usize>` with explicit `any_key_spec(FnId)
-> Option<&FnTypes>` for the handful of typer unit tests that read
`mt[idx].vars`. Drop the field, drop `narrowed_lub` aggregation
(lines 421-426 + the lub mutations in walk_spec_for_discovery),
drop the legacy `iter`/`len` surface.

Tests: full suite. Cleanup only — no behavioral diff.

## Out of scope

  - Refining CallClosure points-to (propagating narrow arg-Descrs
    from unresolved-target CallClosure sites into lambda specs). A
    separate ticket; orthogonal to bootstrap removal.
  - Removing `widen()` / WIDEN_AT. Termination still needs it.
  - Per-fn key budget (alternative termination guard). Not needed;
    SCC + widen already handles it.

## Acceptance

  1. `count` in tail_recursion has exactly two specs:
     `[100000, 0]` and `[int, int]`. No `[int|float, int|float]`.
  2. `lambda_3` in closure_typed_captures has exactly one spec:
     `[10, 20, any]`.
  3. Every spec `(F, K)` in the resulting `module_types.specs` is
     reachable from `entry_seeds(m)` by repeated callsite discovery.
     Sanity-checkable property: a transitive-reachability assertion
     test on every fixture in GOLDEN_FIXTURES.
  4. `cargo test` — all lib + integration tests pass. The only
     accepted golden diff is golden_specs, eyeballed for "every
     dropped spec was orphan."
  5. codegen emits one body per registered spec; sentinel pad
     slots match the FnId-not-in-specs case (.29.12.6 path).
title: fz-typer.reachability-driven-specs: kill the phantom any-key bootstrap
